summary: Reporter requests that the private GRContext field in the Blazor SKGLView be exposed as a public property, matching every other GL view across all platforms (macOS, iOS, Android, WPF, WinForms, WinUI, Tizen, Uno, MAUI).

stepsToReproduce: Compare SKGLView.razor.cs in Blazor (GRContext is private) with any other platform's SKGLView (GRContext is public)
Attempt to access GRContext from the Blazor SKGLView instance — no public API exists
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Blazor WASM, comparing with macOS SKGLView

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The GRContext field in SKGLView.razor.cs is still private as of the current main branch.

analysis.summary: The Blazor SKGLView is the only GL view in SkiaSharp that does not expose GRContext as a public property. Every other platform — macOS, iOS, Android, WPF, WinForms, WinUI, Tizen, Uno, and MAUI — exposes it. The reporter needs GRContext to create additional GPU-accelerated surfaces for rendering. A partial workaround exists via e.Surface.Context within the paint handler.
analysis.rationale: Classified as enhancement rather than bug because the Blazor view functions correctly for basic rendering — it's a missing convenience property, not broken behavior. However, this is a clear platform parity gap: the same API pattern is public on all 10+ other GL view implementations. The fix is trivial (one-line property addition), making it a strong candidate for quick resolution.

keySignals.text: On macOS we can directly access the GRContext of the view
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter is porting from Xamarin.Forms macOS to Blazor and hit a missing API.

keySignals.text: In the Blazor view this is not an option since the field is private
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms the field exists but is not accessible — a simple visibility change would resolve this.

keySignals.text: i'm using the GRContext of the view to generate an additional surface for rendering
keySignals.interpretation: Valid use case — creating secondary GPU surfaces requires sharing the same GRContext.

codeInvestigation.finding: GRContext field is declared as 'private GRContext? context;' — no public accessor exists
macOS exposes 'public GRContext GRContext => context;' — the pattern reporter expects
GRContext extends GRRecordingContext, confirming the workaround via SKSurface.Context (which returns GRRecordingContext) requires a downcast
SKSurface.Context returns GRRecordingContext (base class), so e.Surface.Context as GRContext works but only inside the paint callback

workarounds: Within OnPaintSurface, use pattern matching to safely obtain GRContext: if (e.Surface.Context is GRContext grContext) { ... } — only accessible during the paint callback, not outside it.
nextQuestions: Should the Blazor SKGLView also expose glInterface and renderTarget for advanced users?
Should this follow the MAUI pattern (caching in lastGRContext) to allow access outside paint?

resolution.hypothesis: The private visibility was likely an oversight — every other GL view exposes GRContext publicly.

proposals.title: Add public GRContext property to Blazor SKGLView
proposals.description: Add 'public GRContext? GRContext => context;' to SKGLView.razor.cs, matching the pattern used by all other GL views.

proposals.title: Access GRContext via Surface.Context in paint handler
proposals.description: As a workaround, use pattern matching to safely obtain GRContext from e.Surface.Context inside the OnPaintSurface callback. This only works within the paint handler, not outside it.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add public GRContext property to Blazor SKGLView
resolution.recommendedReason: One-line change that achieves full platform parity. Every other GL view already exposes this property.

actionability.reason: Straightforward enhancement with a trivial fix, but needs maintainer decision on whether to also expose glInterface/renderTarget and whether to use the MAUI caching pattern.

actions.description: Apply enhancement, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Acknowledge the parity gap and provide workaround
